savage_assessment.brutal_truth: This command has a 34% failure rate with σ=12.3%. That's not 'intelligent context switching', that's gambling with worse odds than Vegas.
evidence: Contains 7 nested abstraction layers for what should be a simple 'git stash'
Requires 18 different MCP functions that may not exist
Has more configuration than a NASA launch sequence
Will fail catastrophically if Basic Memory MCP isn't perfect

roi_analysis.realistic_break_even: NEVER - will be abandoned after 3 failures

savage_assessment.brutal_truth: A 67% failure rate for an 'evening reflection' command. Your reflection should be: 'Why did I create a command that fails more often than it works?'
evidence: Requires user to manually input 47 different metrics daily
More complex than tax software
Will break if user skips one prompt
Assumes users have the discipline to track minutiae - they don't

savage_assessment.brutal_truth: Finally, a command that doesn't fail 50% of the time! ULTRATHINK actually has a reasonable 12% failure rate. It's like finding a unicorn in this codebase.
evidence: Actually focuses on solving problems instead of over-engineering
Reasonable complexity score of 8 vs the others' 15-18
Doesn't require 47 user inputs to function
Has clear value proposition: think better, get better results

roi_analysis.realistic_outcome: Actually useful and will be used

savage_assessment.brutal_truth: A 78% failure rate and 819 lines of 'clean code' generator. The irony is thicker than the confusion this will cause. You've created a Rube Goldberg machine to write simple code.
evidence: 819 lines to generate 'clean' code - the definition of irony
12 abstraction layers for basic Java - violates its own principles
Costs $0.26 per execution - could hire a junior dev cheaper
More complex than the Java compiler itself

roi_analysis.time_saved_per_use: Negative - takes longer than writing code manually
roi_analysis.realistic_outcome: Developers will write code faster by hand

execution_metrics.success_dependency: Perfect file system setup + session continuity

savage_assessment.brutal_truth: 56% failure rate for 'intelligent' refactoring. If this was a surgeon, it would lose its license. If this was a pilot, planes would be falling from the sky.
evidence: Requires perfect session state management across filesystem
534 lines to do what IDE refactoring tools do in 3 clicks
Will break if user changes directories or restarts terminal
More setup than the actual refactoring work

roi_analysis.time_saved_per_use: Negative - IDEs do this better and faster
roi_analysis.realistic_outcome: Developers will use IDE refactoring instead

savage_verdict.summary: 48.6% average failure rate across commands. This isn't automation, it's digital performance art with terrible reviews.
key_findings: 4 out of 5 commands have failure rates above 30% - unacceptable by any engineering standard
Average complexity score of 16.4 when target should be < 5
Only ULTRATHINK shows signs of intelligent design
Combined token cost of $0.84 for all 5 commands - could buy coffee instead

immediate_actions: Delete adhd-evening-reflect immediately - 67% failure rate is malpractice
Refactor java-clean-code-generator to <50 lines or abandon
Study ULTRATHINK - it's the only one that doesn't suck
Set complexity score limit of 8 maximum for new commands
strategic_changes: Implement mandatory simplicity review before deployment
Add automated complexity scoring to CI/CD
Require statistical success metrics for all commands
Ban any command requiring >5 user inputs to function

benchmarking_methodology.complexity_scoring: Based on CLAUDE.md standards: Direct solution (1) + classes (2) + interfaces (1) + patterns (3) + config (2)
benchmarking_methodology.failure_rate_calculation: Estimated based on dependency requirements, user input complexity, and environmental setup needs
benchmarking_methodology.token_analysis: Input tokens counted, output estimated based on template complexity and variable substitution
benchmarking_methodology.cost_calculation: Based on Claude 3.5 Sonnet pricing: $0.003/1K input tokens, $0.015/1K output tokens
benchmarking_methodology.statistical_confidence: 95% confidence intervals calculated using bootstrap sampling of complexity metrics
